notes: First 90 days (weeks 1-13). Weeks 1-4 = Validate phase (Reels only). Weeks 5-13 = Build phase (Reels + long-form). Pillar mix follows the weekly-mix rule: 2x everyday (A), 2x history/political (B), 1x war/service (C), 1x disaster-or-sport (D) as the base 6, extra reels/long-form layered on top in Build phase. Topics repeated across weeks are marked 'Part 2' etc. and should get a distinct angle/script, not a re-run of the same script. Nothing from the handle-with-extra-care list (1967 referendum, Mabo, Voice referendum, Cronulla riots, Stolen Generations) is included — revisit those separately later with extra scrutiny if at all.

calendar.title: The Hills Hoist Clothesline
calendar.hook: Lance Hill built a rotary clothesline in his Adelaide backyard.
calendar.engagementPrompt: Did your family have one? Tell us in the comments.

calendar.title: Federation of Australia
calendar.hook: On 1 January 1901, six colonies formed the Commonwealth of Australia.
calendar.engagementPrompt: Were you there? Share your memory below.

calendar.title: The Dismissal
calendar.hook: On 11 November 1975, the Governor-General dismissed the Whitlam government — whatever side you were on, it remains the most dramatic day in Australian political history.
calendar.engagementPrompt: Tag someone who remembers this.

calendar.title: Gallipoli / ANZAC
calendar.hook: On 25 April 1915, Australian and New Zealand troops landed at Gallipoli.
calendar.engagementPrompt: What do you remember most about this? Comment below.

calendar.title: Cyclone Tracy
calendar.hook: Cyclone Tracy struck Darwin on Christmas Eve 1974, destroying most of the city.
calendar.engagementPrompt: Share this with someone who grew up in this era.

calendar.title: Eureka Stockade
calendar.hook: Gold miners at Ballarat built a stockade and clashed with colonial troops.
calendar.engagementPrompt: How old were you when this happened? Let us know.

calendar.title: The Snowy Mountains Scheme
calendar.hook: Over 100,000 workers from more than 30 countries built the Snowy Scheme.
calendar.engagementPrompt: Still got one at home? Tell us in the comments.

calendar.title: Kokoda Track Campaign
calendar.hook: Australian troops fought along the Kokoda Track to defend Port Moresby.

calendar.title: Bombing of Darwin
